turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the Alphabet Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q1 2024, Revenue of $38.1B represented a 28% increase year over year, consistent with our guidance. Our Gemini AI strategy continues to gain traction, with Waymo showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in Gemini AI because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from advertising validate our approach. We are maintaining our full-year guidance and continue to invest in long-term growth opportunities.

Thank you, Sundar. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $111.8 billion, representing growth of 15% year over year. Our search revenue reached $18.4 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 42.9%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $27.9 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $3 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Google Search, we saw 21% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Google Search?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On search revenue, we are pleased with the progress on cloud profitability and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Google Search to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about search innovation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Toni. Great question. On search revenue, we are pleased with the progress on search innovation and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Google Search to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Pixel?

Sure, Keith. The search revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about search innovation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Brad. The YouTube ad revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Alphabet Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a challenging quarter as we navigated headwinds across several of our markets. In Q2 2024, search revenue came in below expectations, reflecting ongoing challenges in the current environment. Our cloud profitability strategy continues to gain traction, with Google Search showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in cloud profitability because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from AI infrastructure validate our approach. We expect near-term headwinds to persist but believe our strategic positioning will drive recovery.

Thank you, Sundar. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $120.8 billion, representing growth of 2% year over year. Our search revenue reached $12.8 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 35.4%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $5.7 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $13 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Google Search, we saw 7% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about search innovation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On TAC rate, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position YouTube for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect YouTube to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Pixel?

Sure, Amit. The YouTube ad revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On YouTube, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Toni. The cloud revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On YouTube, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Wamsi. The TAC rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about cloud profitability. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Karl. The cloud revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Alphabet Inc. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am extremely pleased with our performance this quarter. The momentum we are seeing is unprecedented. In Q3 2024, We saw 3% growth in Pixel, well above our internal targets. Our search innovation strategy continues to gain traction, with Pixel showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in search innovation because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from AI infrastructure validate our approach. We are increasingly confident in our ability to sustain this growth trajectory into the next fiscal year.

Thank you, Sundar. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $104.8 billion, representing growth of 17% year over year. Our search revenue reached $31.3 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 23.1%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $7.3 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Google Search, we saw 13% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Wamsi. The TAC rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the search revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Amit. The search revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Google Cloud?

Sure, Shannon. The cloud revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Google Cloud?

Sure, Karl. The cloud revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Toni. The search revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Alphabet Inc. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This quarter demonstrated resilience across our business despite a mixed macroeconomic environment. In Q4 2024, Revenue of $12.1B represented a 22% increase year over year, consistent with our guidance. Our search innovation strategy continues to gain traction, with Pixel showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in search innovation because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from cloud platform validate our approach. We are maintaining our full-year guidance and continue to invest in long-term growth opportunities.

Thank you, Sundar. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $115.5 billion, representing growth of 7% year over year. Our search revenue reached $39.8 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 28.2%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $28.7 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $15 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Google Search, we saw 5% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Karl. The TAC rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On YouTube, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Toni. The search revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Google Search?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On TAC rate, we are pleased with the progress on Gemini AI and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Google Search to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On YouTube, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Brad. Great question. On TAC rate, we are pleased with the progress on cost efficiency and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect YouTube to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Alphabet Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a record-breaking quarter for the company, reflecting the strength of our strategy and execution. In Q2 2025, Our TAC rate reached an all-time high of $63.3B, driven by strong demand across all regions. Our cost efficiency strategy continues to gain traction, with YouTube showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in cost efficiency because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from AI infrastructure validate our approach. We are increasingly confident in our ability to sustain this growth trajectory into the next fiscal year.

Thank you, Sundar. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2025 was $95.3 billion, representing a decline of 2% year over year. Our search revenue reached $12.7 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin was 34.3%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $13.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Google Search, we saw 16% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On YouTube ad revenue, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in search innovation has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Waymo to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Google Cloud?

Sure, Toni. The TAC rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the TAC rate trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Brent. Great question. On TAC rate, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in search innovation has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Google Search to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On YouTube ad revenue, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in cost efficiency has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Google Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Ruth for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Sure, Amit. The TAC rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
